title: mldsa sign uses Ffi error variant for caller-side size check
description: MlDsaKey::sign returns WolfHsmError::Ffi when msg.len() exceeds u32::MAX, but this is a pure Rust-side argument validation — no FFI call has been made. The Ffi variant signals that a C function returned an error code, so this misclassification will confuse callers inspecting the error. Should be WolfHsmError::BadArgs { msg: "message exceeds u32::MAX bytes" } to match every other size-check in the codebase.
